message:
test(resolv): POSIX services(5) conformance table (bd-xoqs)

Add spec-derived conformance table (Pattern 4) for parse_services_line
with 12 cases citing services(5) man-page clauses:

  SERVICES-PARSE-001   canonical format: name\\tport/protocol
  SERVICES-PARSE-002   multi-alias line
  SERVICES-PARSE-003   udp protocol accepted
  SERVICES-PARSE-004   inline '#' comment stripped
  SERVICES-PARSE-005   pure comment line rejected
  SERVICES-PARSE-006   blank line rejected
  SERVICES-PARSE-007   missing '/' in port/proto rejected
  SERVICES-PARSE-008   empty protocol after '/' rejected
  SERVICES-PARSE-009   non-numeric port rejected
  SERVICES-PARSE-010   port > 65535 rejected (u16 overflow)
  SERVICES-PARSE-011   boundary: port 0 valid
  SERVICES-PARSE-012   boundary: port 65535 valid

Complements the existing smoke-fuzz coverage (bd-s170) by pinning
spec provenance for each documented grammar rule. Together with
hosts(5) (bd-x7jd) and resolver(5) (bd-x7jd) tables this closes the
NSS files-backend parser conformance triad (/etc/hosts,
/etc/resolv.conf, /etc/services).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-w2c3.9.1", "title": "T8.1 Unit Test Closure Packs by Family", "description": "## Background
Build exhaustive unit packs for remaining weak/complex families with adversarial and regression coverage.

## Design
Cover startup, loader edges, resolver/NSS, locale/iconv, signal/setjmp, SysV IPC, unwinding/session paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
All packs include strict/hardened cases where relevant and explicit expected invariants.

## Notes
Output per-pack coverage and failure diagnostics with trace IDs.", "design": "Cover startup, loader edges, resolver/NSS, locale/iconv, signal/setjmp, SysV IPC, unwinding/session paths.", "acceptance_criteria": "All packs include strict/hardened cases where relevant and explicit expected invariants.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Tooling hard requirement applies: use `/dp/asupersync` for deterministic orchestration/traceability and `/dp/frankentui` for deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI analysis output.", "notes": "Output per-pack coverage and failure diagnostics with trace IDs.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set documented in bead comments/notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

Tooling contract: closure requires evidence of `/dp/asupersync` orchestration/traceability integration and `/dp/frankentui` deterministic diff/snapshot analysis integration.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:15:30.722377618Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T18:29:37.156901233Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T18:29:37.156878831Z", "close_reason": "Unit test closure packs complete: 7 new fixtures (49 cases), 12 required families covered, CI gate + 4 harness tests pass. 48 total fixtures, 470 cases.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["execution", "feature-parity", "gap-closure"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.8.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.9", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1213, "issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "CLAIMING: PearlFinch starting bd-w2c3.9.1. Will build unit test closure packs for remaining weak/complex families with strict/hardened coverage. bd-w2c3.8.1 is CLOSED, unblocked.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T18:26:04Z"}, {"id": 1214, "issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "DONE: Created 7 new closure pack fixtures (setjmp_ops, sysv_ipc_ops, backtrace_ops, session_ops, loader_edges, spawn_exec_ops, regex_glob_ops) totaling 49 new cases all with strict+hardened mode coverage. CI gate check_unit_test_packs.sh validates 12 required families. 4 Rust harness tests pass. Total: 48 fixture files, 470 cases, 64% symbol coverage. Closing. —PearlFinch", "created_at": "2026-02-13T18:29:35Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "title": "T8.2 Deterministic E2E Packs: smoke/stress/fault/stability/security", "description": "## Background
Expand deterministic e2e packs with strict+hardened pair-runs, replay controls, and failure triage bundles.

## Design
Scenario manifest includes normal workloads, adversarial misuse, long-run stability, and overload paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
Pair-run comparator reports strict-vs-hardened divergence with bounded/expected rationale.

## Notes
Artifact bundle includes logs, diffs, metrics, crash bundles, and replay command lines.", "design": "Scenario manifest includes normal workloads, adversarial misuse, long-run stability, and overload paths.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Pair-run comparator reports strict-vs-hardened divergence with bounded/expected rationale.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Mandatory build-tooling contract enforced: deterministic conformance orchestration and traceability must use `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence surfaces must use `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "Artifact bundle includes logs, diffs, metrics, crash bundles, and replay command lines.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set documented in bead comments/notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

Tooling contract: this bead cannot close without explicit evidence that `/dp/asupersync` is used for deterministic orchestration/traceability and `/dp/frankentui` is used for deterministic diff/snapshot-oriented analysis output.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:15:30.981800759Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T23:02:47.242268615Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T23:02:47.242032247Z", "close_reason": "Deliverables present in working tree from swarm + direct work.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["execution", "feature-parity", "gap-closure"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.4.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.7.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.8.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.8.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.9", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.9.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "title": "T8.3 Structured Log/Evidence Unification + Artifact Index", "description": "## Background
Unify runtime decision logs, evidence ledger records, conformance outputs, and e2e artifacts into one queryable index.

## Design
Schema guarantees join keys across trace_id/span_id/controller_id/policy_id/evidence_seqno.

## Acceptance Criteria
Index enables one-command triage from failing bead/gate to all relevant artifacts.

## Notes
Add schema-validation tests and backward-compatibility migration checks.", "design": "Schema guarantees join keys across trace_id/span_id/controller_id/policy_id/evidence_seqno.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Index enables one-command triage from failing bead/gate to all relevant artifacts.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Mandatory build-tooling contract enforced: deterministic conformance orchestration and traceability must use `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence surfaces must use `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "Add schema-validation tests and backward-compatibility migration checks.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set documented in bead comments/notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

Tooling contract: this bead cannot close without explicit evidence that `/dp/asupersync` is used for deterministic orchestration/traceability and `/dp/frankentui` is used for deterministic diff/snapshot-oriented analysis output.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:15:31.241516216Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-06T23:01:52.727948028Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-06T23:01:52.727687585Z", "close_reason": "Implemented shared artifact-index compatibility and schema/migration coverage", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["execution", "feature-parity", "gap-closure"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.2.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.7.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.9", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.9.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "depends_on_id": "bd-w2c3.9.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1394, "issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "author": "OlivePuma", "text": "Bounded partial-unblock slice landed in frankenlibc-harness shadow-run: added artifact-index output + canonical trace_id propagation for scenario reports/logs/artifacts, wired , and extended shadow-run tests to assert artifact-index join keys. Verification via rch: 
running 8 tests
test manifest_identifies_shadow_scenarios ... ok
test executor_writes_requested_artifacts ... ok
test nonzero_allowed_exit_code_does_not_trigger_baseline_error ... ok
test missing_required_artifact_fails_shadow_manifest ... ok
test smoke_fixture_placeholder_is_resolved_into_replay_command ... ok
test optional_missing_binary_is_skipped_instead_of_aborting_manifest ... ok
test expected_divergence_can_satisfy_shadow_scenario_contract ... ok
test run_shadow_manifest_writes_report_log_and_replay_bundle ... ok

test result: ok. 8 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.12s PASS;  PASS;  PASS. Remaining non-slice failures: workspace-wide Diff in /data/projects/frankenlibc/crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/ebr_integration_test.rs:359:
         d.total_reclaimed <= d.total_retired,
         "cannot reclaim more than retired"
     );
[31m-    assert!(d.global_epoch > 0, "mixed workload should advance the global epoch");
(B[m[32m+    assert!(
(B[m[32m+        d.global_epoch > 0,
(B[m[32m+        "mixed workload should advance the global epoch"
(B[m[32m+    );
(B[m     assert_eq!(
         d.pending_per_epoch.iter().sum::<usize>(),
         0, still fails on pre-existing formatting in ;  still fails on pre-existing dead_code in .", "created_at": "2026-04-06T21:43:07Z"}, {"id": 1395, "issue_id": "bd-w2c3.9.3", "author": "ubuntu", "text": "Completed shared artifact-index compatibility + schema coverage on top of the shadow-run slice.

Changes:
- structured_log now accepts legacy artifact-index join-key aliases (`trace_id`, `span_id`, `decision_id`, `policy_id`, `evidence_seqno`) and normalizes them into canonical plural arrays.
- artifact indexes missing `run_id`/`generated_utc` now deserialize with deterministic legacy defaults via `normalize_legacy_defaults()`.
- evidence_compliance emits a WARN proof-log event (`evidence_compliance.artifact_index_legacy_defaults`) when legacy defaults are synthesized, instead of failing older v1 bundles.
- added schema/migration coverage in `structured_log_test` and `evidence_compliance_test`, including the canonical `log_schema.json` artifact-index example and a legacy index without `run_id`.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test structured_log_test --test evidence_compliance_test`
  Expected: 17 focused tests pass.
  Observed: PASS (6 evidence-compliance tests + 11 structured-log tests).
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
  Expected: workspace check passes.
  Observed: PASS.
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo fmt --check`
  Observed: FAIL due pre-existing formatting drift in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` (not this slice).
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  Observed: FAIL due pre-existing `clippy::manual_is_multiple_of` in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/alien_cs_e2e_test.rs:170` and `:173` (outside this slice).

Failure triage:
1. Legacy artifact-index bundles missing `run_id` should now produce a WARN proof-log event, not an `artifact_index.invalid_json` parse failure. Inspect `evidence_compliance.proof.log.jsonl`.
2. Singular legacy join-key fields should serialize back out as canonical plural arrays. Inspect `structured_log_test` roundtrip output if this regresses.
3. Remaining workspace fmt/clippy failures are unrelated repo drift; inspect the files above before blaming this bead.", "created_at": "2026-04-06T23:01:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wtlg","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:51:44.009148022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T19:10:05.779454951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T19:10:05.776806248Z","close_reason":"Current tree already fixed: io_internal_abi uses stdio_abi tmpfile/fclose, replacement guard passes, and standalone cargo check succeeds","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-x7jd","title":"POSIX/hosts(5) conformance table for /etc/hosts parser","description":"frankenlibc-core::resolv has unit tests for parse_hosts_line/lookup_hosts but no spec-annotated conformance table. Add Pattern-4 spec-derived table with cases citing hosts(5) + RFC 952 + RFC 1123 (hostname validation). Coverage: whitespace (spaces/tabs/mixed), case insensitivity, IPv4/IPv6 mix, multi-alias lines, comments (start/mid-line), blank lines, CRLF, malformed addresses, RFC 952/1123 hostname char set. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module. One commit per section group.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:25:33.676462834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:29:43.474768096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:29:43.472712968Z","close_reason":"Added 3 POSIX/resolver(5)/hosts(5) spec-derived conformance tables across 2 commits: ad881c2 (hosts parse 16 cases + lookup 7 cases), 0b464ba (resolv.conf 12 cases). Total 35 cases with spec_ref citations for hosts(5), RFC 952, RFC 1123, RFC 1035, resolver(5), and <resolv.h> MAXNS cap.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xkc6","title":"POSIX §7.3 conformance table for ctype classification/conversion","description":"ctype::mod has good proptest invariants (prop_core_classification, case_conversion_roundtrip, to_ascii_masks) but no spec-derived table citing POSIX.1-2017 §7.3.1. Add Pattern-4 conformance table with explicit clause refs for is_alpha/is_digit/is_space/is_xdigit/is_cntrl/is_print/is_punct/is_blank and the to_upper/to_lower mapping rules. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ctype/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:40:33.338358031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:42:33.032693512Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:42:33.030520049Z","close_reason":"Added 2 POSIX §7.3 conformance tables in commit 3d7aa71: classification (10 predicates × in/out-of-class sweeps) + case-mapping (full 256-byte sweep asserting alpha mapping + identity on non-alpha). All cases cite IEEE 1003.1-2017 §7.3.1 or §7.3.2 clauses.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-xoqs","title":"POSIX services(5) conformance table for parse_services_line","description":"parse_services_line in frankenlibc_core::resolv has smoke-fuzz coverage (bd-s170) and unit tests but no spec-annotated conformance table. Add Pattern-4 conformance table citing services(5) man page clauses — service-name format, port/protocol grammar, aliases, comments. Complements the existing hosts(5) + resolver(5) conformance suite (bd-x7jd).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T21:24:38.654768153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T21:24:44.145851642Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-xxd9", "title": "bd-25n subtask: Threading/futex lifecycle exhaustive unit+stress test packs", "description": "Background:
- Threading and synchronization logic remains a critical defect surface.

Goal:
- Build exhaustive unit test packs for pthread/futex/thread bootstrap lifecycle semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Mutex/condvar/thread/join/detach API-level unit tests.
2) Concurrency adversarial tests with deterministic scheduling controls where feasible.
3) Regression suite for known deadlock/hang signatures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Threading test packs cover success, misuse, contention, timeout, teardown paths.
- Regression signatures are pinned and automatically checked.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit and stress test scripts.
- Structured logs with thread/event timelines and failure markers.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "DustyRiver", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:02:38.991066758Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-16T14:34:54.992244359Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-16T14:34:54.992224261Z", "close_reason": "Thread/futex lifecycle pack now includes mutex/condvar/rwlock/once/tsd + lifecycle regressions (including self-join). Validated via targeted rch test binaries and workspace quality gates.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "pthread", "testing", "unit", "verification"], "comments": [{"id": 1217, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9", "author": "DustyRiver", "text": "Taking ownership of `bd-xxd9` based on `bv` robot triage.

Triage evidence (robot mode):
- `bv --robot-next` top pick surfaced `bd-rqn` (already in progress).
- `bv --robot-triage` critical path shows `bd-25n` as top impact node, blocked by `bd-xxd9`.
- `bd-25n` currently unblocks 10 downstream issues, so advancing `bd-xxd9` is highest-impact actionable unblock.

Execution start:
- Reassigned `bd-xxd9` to `DustyRiver` and keeping status `in_progress`.
- Starting with release-mode pthread regression signatures already reproduced under LD_PRELOAD:
  - `python3_print` timeout
  - `coreutils_ls_tmp` segfault
  - `sort/wc/redis` timeouts
- Will add deterministic unit+stress coverage and structured artifact references as evidence.
", "created_at": "2026-02-16T06:19:58Z"}, {"id": 1218, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Progress update for `bd-xxd9` (threading/futex lifecycle unit+stress packs):

Implemented in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`:

 {"id": "bd-xxd9.1", "title": "Native pthread self-join can deadlock under thread lifecycle path", "description": "Observed while developing bd-xxd9 regression tests: a child thread invoking pthread_join on its own pthread_t can hang indefinitely instead of returning EDEADLK. Repro path came from an ABI test that passed self handle via shared slot and called pthread_join(self). This can wedge the lifecycle suite (global test guard then blocks subsequent tests). Need root-cause fix in native join/create lifecycle path plus deterministic non-hanging regression.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "DustyRiver", "created_at": "2026-02-16T07:04:33.822441437Z", "created_by": "DustyRiver", "updated_at": "2026-02-16T14:31:41.100312883Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-16T14:31:41.100295009Z", "close_reason": "Self-join deadlock hardened: core+ABI guards in place; regression test re-enabled in default lifecycle suite and validated via repeated rch runs + workspace gates.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["pthread", "regression", "threading"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-xxd9.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-xxd9", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1223, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "`bd-xxd9.1` investigation update (self-join deadlock):

Repro status:
- Repro remains valid: explicit self-join regression (`pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk`) can hang >60s when run standalone.
- This is why the self-join regression remains excluded from default suite (no default CI wedging).

What I tested:
1. Parent-side `tid` publication in `create_thread` (store returned child tid before startup wait).
2. Child-side early `tid` publication in trampoline.
3. Removed `std::thread::yield_now()` from helper (to avoid clone/TLS interaction suspicion) in explicit repro path.

Outcome:
- None of the above alone eliminated the explicit self-join hang in this environment.
- Default lifecycle suite remains stable with stress tests opt-in/ignored.

Current safe posture:
- Keep self-join issue tracked as `bd-xxd9.1` (in_progress).
- Keep non-hanging lifecycle/condvar regressions active in default runs.
- Keep stress/endurance paths opt-in (`#[ignore]`) until self-join path is resolved.

Validation re-confirmed during this pass:
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed, 5 ignored`)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`7 passed`)
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
  (all run through `rch exec -- ...`; artifact retrieval warnings from worker are environmental and occurred after successful remote command exit)
", "created_at": "2026-02-16T07:24:14Z"}, {"id": 1224, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Progress update (self-join hardening pass):

Implemented:
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs`: added `ThreadHandle.self_tid` (stable child-captured identity), early publication of child tid/self_tid in trampoline, and an additional running-state self-join guard in `join_thread` before futex wait paths.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs`: added ABI-level early self-join rejection in `native_pthread_join`:
  - direct `thread == my_tid` guard for fallback raw-TID self IDs
  - registry-backed check against target handle `tid/self_tid` while `THREAD_STARTING|THREAD_RUNNING` before handle removal/core join call.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`: kept `pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk` as explicit opt-in (`#[ignore]`) due remaining scheduler-sensitive startup window during this bead; avoids default-suite wedge while hardening continues.

Validation (using rch for cargo test/check/clippy):
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed, 6 ignored`)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`7 passed`)
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅

Status:
- Deadlock incidence reduced in explicit probes, but startup-order nondeterminism still appears in repeated forced self-join probes; keeping this bug in-progress for another pass to make self-join assertion fully deterministic and unignore regression.
", "created_at": "2026-02-16T14:08:24Z"}, {"id": 1225, "issue_id": "bd-xxd9.1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "New execution slice started from `bv --robot-triage`:

- `bd-25n` remains highest-impact node but blocked by `bd-xxd9`.
- `bd-xxd9.1` remains the active blocker bug in this lane.

This slice focus:
1) Remove startup-order nondeterminism in self-join path (`pthread_join(pthread_self())`).
2) Drive deterministic behavior toward strict `EDEADLK` in explicit probe runs.
3) If stable, unignore `pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk`; otherwise keep it explicit opt-in with updated evidence.

Starting implementation now in:
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs`
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs`
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`
", "created_at": "2026-02-16T14:28:33Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-xxyb", "title": "Investigate Python startup perf regression in LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (51/7/6)", "description": "## Problem
LD_PRELOAD smoke suite regressed from 58/0/6 to 51/7/6. All 7 failures are Python-related with failure_signature=startup_perf_regression. Correctness is fine (parity_pass=true) — only startup latency exceeds the perf gate threshold.

## Failing Cases
- strict: python3_print, stress_python_{1..5}
- hardened: stress_python_5

## Probable Cause
Recent changes to signal handling and malloc that added overhead:
1. signal_abi.rs: Signal trampoline installation, SA_RESTORER marshalling, deferred delivery critical sections
2. malloc_abi.rs: Signal critical section entry/exit around every malloc/calloc/free/realloc call
3. host_resolve.rs: Streaming /proc/self/maps parser changes

Python startup is particularly sensitive because it calls sigaction dozens of times during interpreter init, and does heavy allocation.

## Investigation Steps
1. Profile Python startup under LD_PRELOAD to identify the hotspot
2. Check if the malloc signal critical section is the bottleneck (atomic ops on every alloc)
3. Check if sigaction trampoline installation overhead is significant
4. Consider: make signal critical sections opt-in (only when FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened)

## Acceptance Criteria
Smoke suite returns to 58/0/6 (or 57/0/7 with the new skipped binary). Python startup perf within threshold.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-03T18:43:55.100768393Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-04T00:24:27.961421919Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-04T00:23:57.951709506Z", "close_reason": "Smoke gate back to 58/0/6; strict+hardened Python cases green", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["malloc", "performance", "regression", "signal"], "comments": [{"id": 1367, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "ubuntu", "text": "Investigation finding: strace -c output is garbled under LD_PRELOAD because our printf engine does NOT support POSIX positional format specifiers (%n$s, %n$d). strace uses these extensively for column-aligned output. This is a separate correctness bug from the perf regression. The perf regression itself appears to be from the 275ms Python startup (vs 15ms native) — likely caused by signal trampoline overhead per sigaction call (67 calls) and /proc/self/maps reads (6 reads). The positional printf bug should be a separate bead.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T18:46:53Z"}, {"id": 1368, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "ubuntu", "text": "Root cause analysis: The 275ms Python startup (vs 15ms native) is primarily from host symbol resolution overhead during early bootstrap, not from the signal trampoline per se. Each of the 67 sigaction calls triggers runtime_policy::decide() + kernel struct marshalling + trampoline installation, but the main cost is likely the resolve_host_symbol_raw calls reading /proc/self/maps (6 reads confirmed via strace). The signal trampoline itself is cheap after first install. Possible fixes: (1) cache /proc/self/maps content, (2) lazy-resolve symbols on first actual use not on every sigaction, (3) batch symbol resolution at startup. This is an optimization task, not a correctness bug.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T18:47:54Z"}, {"id": 1374, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "LavenderMarsh", "text": "First perf datapoint after strict allocator fast-path reorder in malloc_abi.rs: rebuilt release lib via rch (cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release) and remeasured local python3 -c print(1) startup over 5 runs. Baseline avg ~16.99 ms, strict avg ~61.11 ms, down from the previous ~290.72 ms on the pre-patch release artifact. This strongly suggests the strict-mode startup regression was paying allocator signal/policy scaffolding on the host path. Awaiting the post-patch hardened measurement before deciding whether sigaction needs a separate fast path for bd-xxyb.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T20:12:57Z"}, {"id": 1375, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "LavenderMarsh", "text": "Second perf fix in progress: added a strict-mode fast path in signal_abi::sigaction so strict mode skips runtime_policy::decide/observe entirely and goes straight to validation + rt_sigaction marshalling, since strict mode never repairs signal installs. This targets the remaining Python startup tax after the allocator host-path reorder.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T20:14:40Z"}, {"id": 1376, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "LavenderMarsh", "text": "Fixed strict-mode Python startup regression in two steps. 1) host-thread pthread/TLS split in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs: host-created threads now use host pthread_self, pthread_key_{create,delete}, and pthread_{get,set}specific; fresh strict preload dropped gettid calls from ~2252 to 2. 2) strict-mode raw fast paths for memcpy/strlen in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs so hot string ops skip runtime_policy::decide in strict mode; fresh local 5-run sample against /tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so improved strict startup from ~58 ms to ~37 ms. Current strict syscall profile: gettid=2, clock_gettime=142, futex=135. Verification via rch: cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed; cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo fmt --check clean.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T20:32:05Z"}, {"id": 1377, "issue_id": "bd-xxyb", "author": "LavenderMarsh", "text": "Closure evidence: rebuilt fresh release artifact via rch into /data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so, then ran the real smoke gate with an explicit pinned lib path after fixing scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh to honor FRANKENLIBC_SMOKE_LIB_PATH. Command: rch exec -- env FRANKENLIBC_SMOKE_LIB_PATH=/data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target TIMEOUT_SECONDS=10 STRESS_ITERS=5 bash scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh. Result: Summary passes=58 fails=0 skips=6, artifact bundle at target/ld_preload_smoke/20260404T002003Z. All strict and hardened Python cases passed, including python3_print and stress_python_{1..5}. This satisfies bd-xxyb acceptance criteria.", "created_at": "2026-04-04T00:21:07Z"}]}

@@ -1267,6 +1267,177 @@ mod tests {
             }
         }
 
+        /// POSIX services(5) conformance — cited spec clauses below.
+        /// Each case pins a specific documented behavior of the
+        /// services-file parser. Keeps the invariant proptests
+        /// (fuzz_parse_services_line_*) as the "does it crash / is
+        /// output a subset" fence and adds spec provenance here.
+        #[test]
+        fn services_parse_conformance_table(
+            // Use any() for proptest's boilerplate; we ignore the arg
+            // and run the full const table once per iteration with
+            // cases=1 effectively.
+            _seed in any::<u8>(),
+        ) {
+            struct Case {
+                id: &'static str,
+                spec_ref: &'static str,
+                input: &'static [u8],
+                expected_name: Option<&'static [u8]>,
+                expected_port: u16,
+                expected_proto: &'static [u8],
+                expected_aliases: &'static [&'static [u8]],
+            }
+            const TABLE: &[Case] = &[
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-001",
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — official_name port/protocol",
+                    input: b"ssh\t22/tcp",
+                    expected_name: Some(b"ssh"),
+                    expected_port: 22,
+                    expected_proto: b"tcp",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-002",
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — multiple aliases allowed",
+                    input: b"http  80/tcp  www www-http",
+                    expected_name: Some(b"http"),
+                    expected_port: 80,
+                    expected_proto: b"tcp",
+                    expected_aliases: &[b"www", b"www-http"],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-003",
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — udp protocol accepted",
+                    input: b"dns 53/udp",
+                    expected_name: Some(b"dns"),
+                    expected_port: 53,
+                    expected_proto: b"udp",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-004",
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Description — '#' starts comment",
+                    input: b"smtp 25/tcp mail  # Simple Mail Transfer",
+                    expected_name: Some(b"smtp"),
+                    expected_port: 25,
+                    expected_proto: b"tcp",
+                    expected_aliases: &[b"mail"],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-005",
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Description — pure comment line rejected",
+                    input: b"# This is a comment",
+                    expected_name: None,
+                    expected_port: 0,
+                    expected_proto: b"",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-006",
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Description — blank line rejected",
+                    input: b"",
+                    expected_name: None,
+                    expected_port: 0,
+                    expected_proto: b"",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-007",
+                    // services(5): port/protocol must include '/' separator.
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — missing '/' in port/proto rejected",
+                    input: b"bad 22tcp",
+                    expected_name: None,
+                    expected_port: 0,
+                    expected_proto: b"",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-008",
+                    // An empty protocol field after '/' is ill-formed.
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — empty protocol rejected",
+                    input: b"bad 22/",
+                    expected_name: None,
+                    expected_port: 0,
+                    expected_proto: b"",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-009",
+                    // Non-numeric port must be rejected (u16::parse fails).
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — non-numeric port rejected",
+                    input: b"bad abc/tcp",
+                    expected_name: None,
+                    expected_port: 0,
+                    expected_proto: b"",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-010",
+                    // Port > 65535 must be rejected (u16 overflow).
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — port must fit u16 (0..=65535)",
+                    input: b"bad 70000/tcp",
+                    expected_name: None,
+                    expected_port: 0,
+                    expected_proto: b"",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-011",
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — boundary: port 0 is valid",
+                    input: b"nullport 0/tcp",
+                    expected_name: Some(b"nullport"),
+                    expected_port: 0,
+                    expected_proto: b"tcp",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+                Case {
+                    id: "SERVICES-PARSE-012",
+                    spec_ref: "services(5) ¶Format — boundary: port 65535 is valid",
+                    input: b"maxport 65535/tcp",
+                    expected_name: Some(b"maxport"),
+                    expected_port: 65535,
+                    expected_proto: b"tcp",
+                    expected_aliases: &[],
+                },
+            ];
+
+            let mut fails = Vec::new();
+            for case in TABLE {
+                let actual = parse_services_line(case.input);
+                let pass = match (&actual, case.expected_name) {
+                    (None, None) => true,
+                    (Some(entry), Some(want_name)) => {
+                        entry.name == want_name
+                            && entry.port == case.expected_port
+                            && entry.protocol == case.expected_proto
+                            && entry.aliases.len() == case.expected_aliases.len()
+                            && entry
+                                .aliases
+                                .iter()
+                                .zip(case.expected_aliases.iter())
+                                .all(|(a, b)| a == b)
+                    }
+                    _ => false,
+                };
+                if !pass {
+                    fails.push(format!(
+                        "{} [{}]: input={:?}, actual={:?}",
+                        case.id,
+                        case.spec_ref,
+                        core::str::from_utf8(case.input).unwrap_or("<non-utf8>"),
+                        actual,
+                    ));
+                }
+            }
+            prop_assert!(
+                fails.is_empty(),
+                "services(5) parse conformance failures:\n  {}",
+                fails.join("\n  ")
+            );
+        }
+
         /// Biased alphabet containing the port-grammar delimiters
         /// ('/') and digit/alpha characters — a raw-byte fuzz almost
         /// never produces a '/' in the right spot to reach the port
